body_txt: Normally in works that center around Foolish Sibling, Responsible Sibling relationships, the older one is portrayed as the mature one who keeps their younger sibling in line, with the latter usually playing the Annoying Younger Sibling role to their Aloof Big Brother . But what happens if the roles are reversed? In that case, you'll get the Mature Younger Sibling, whose main role is to act as the voice of reason to their older sibling, who in turn is either a Childish Older Sibling , Disappointing Older Sibling , a Big Brother Bully , or a combination of all three (or an age-inversion of Annoying Younger Sibling ). The younger sibling may be an Adorably Precocious Child or Wise Beyond Their Years , or maybe just an average kid themselves but has some sort of maturity advantage which causes them to claim seniority over their older sibling. The older sibling may be a Kiddie Kid or Manchild who either acts in a childish way due to a head injury as a baby , a developmental disability, or perhaps they do know better but just decide to act immaturely towards their younger sibling For the Evulz . Important: The older sibling doesn’t have to be childish for this trope. The older sibling can be just as mature if not more mature than the younger sibling; because the younger sibling is more mature for their age than expected, it may only seem like the older sibling acts younger in comparison. In extreme cases, the parents actually trust the younger sibling to look after the older one and babysit for them while they're gone. However, in an optimistic scenario, the older sibling might mature over time either through a personal experience or just being a late bloomer and reclaim power or become equal to their younger sibling, eventually making them not so powerful and perhaps forming some mutual bond towards one another. In the darkest cases , the older sibling is either dangerously mentally unstable with anger issues , a violent type of Insufferable Imbecile , or has had enough of feeling patronized and treated as younger and may flip the dynamics and do messed up and abusive things to the younger sibling so the older sibling can seem powerful and mature in comparison without growing up themselves. To signify the ironic power dynamic, the younger one may be a Big Little Brother while the older one may be a Little Big Brother . Compare this to its older counterpart Cool Big Sis . Contrast Childish Older Sibling , Disappointing Older Sibling , Annoying Younger Sibling , and Oblivious Younger Sibling . If the younger sibling has flaws of their own that cause them to be overpowered by an older person in authority, they're Only Sane by Comparison .
